mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, with trade-offs in terms of complexity and resource requirements. This installation guide will help you set up vLLM on a Kubernetes cluster.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the vLLM command-line interface to interact with the vLLM service.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.7.0

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Provision a new Kubernetes cluster with the required version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure kubectl to use the new cluster's credentials.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes are up and running in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the Ready state.

steps.title: Deploy vLLM
steps.description:
Deploy the vLLM service using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> Add the vLLM Helm repository.
> Install vLLM in the vllm namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for the vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment vllm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace vllm

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Enable autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Apply your monitoring configuration (ensure this file exists).

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
Remove the vLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the deployment.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command removes the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the entire cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Backup your current configuration, upgrade the vLLM deployment, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --namespace vllm > vllm-backup.txt

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl get pods --namespace vllm

```

> Backup current deployment state.
> Upgrade vLLM to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or the tag is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM pod is failing to start due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace vllm' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resource limits in your deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Service not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM service may not be properly deployed or exposed.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is created and check with 'kubectl get services --namespace vllm'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved memory management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for older model formats.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.17.0, ensure to update your model configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace vllm' showing all pods in the Running state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and cloud CLI installed before proceeding.
